content:
Given the polynomial:
\[
P(x) = x^5 + 7x^4 - 2 = 0,
\]
with distinct roots \(a, b, c, d, e\).

We want to find:
\[
S = \sum_{\text{root } r} \frac{r^4}{\prod_{\substack{\text{root } s \neq r}} (r - s)}.
\]

---

### Step 1: Recognize the structure of the denominator

For a polynomial \(P(x)\) of degree \(n\) with distinct roots \(r_1, r_2, \ldots, r_n\), we have:
\[
P(x) = (x - r_1)(x - r_2) \cdots (x - r_n).
\]

The derivative is:
\[
P'(x) = \sum_{i=1}^n \prod_{\substack{j=1 \\ j \neq i}}^n (x - r_j).
\]

Evaluating at \(x = r_i\), all terms vanish except the \(i\)-th term, so:
\[
P'(r_i) = \prod_{\substack{j=1 \\ j \neq i}}^n (r_i - r_j).
\]

Hence,
\[
\prod_{\substack{s \neq r}} (r - s) = P'(r).
\
